module-1-profile-card-project.label: Module Project: Build a Profile Card
module-1-profile-card-project.summary: A tutor wants a quick profile card generator for new students. Build a small program that stores values, reads input, cleans text, and prints a polished profile.

mp_1.title: Store and print the first profile values

mp-1-store-profile-values.title: Store and print the first profile values
mp-1-store-profile-values.prompt: The tutor wants a first draft of the profile card. Store `"Maya"` in `student_name`, store `7` in `level`, then print `student_name` on one line and `level` on the next line.
mp-1-store-profile-values.hint: Use two assignment lines first, then print each variable.

help.concept: A small profile program starts by storing information in clear variables and printing it back out.
help.hint_1: Create the variables before printing them.
help.hint_2: Print the name first and the level second.

mp-1-store-profile-values.starterCode:
# Store the student name and level.
# Then print both values on separate lines.

mp-2-read-and-label-input.title: Read and label input
mp-2-read-and-label-input.prompt:
A student will type a name and a city, one per line. Read both inputs and print exactly:
Name: <name>
City: <city>
mp-2-read-and-label-input.hint: Use `input()` twice, then print each labeled line.

help.concept: Input makes the profile card flexible because the same program can print a different card each time it runs.
help.hint_1: The first input is the name and the second input is the city.
help.hint_2: Print the labels exactly as shown.

mp-2-read-and-label-input.starterCode:
# Read the student's name and city.
# Then print the two labeled lines.

mp-3-clean-typed-name.title: Clean a typed name
mp-3-clean-typed-name.prompt: A student sometimes types a messy name with extra spaces and lowercase letters. Read one line of input, clean it with `.strip()` and `.title()`, and print the cleaned name.
mp-3-clean-typed-name.hint: First remove outside spaces, then change the text to title case.

help.concept: String methods help a small project turn messy input into cleaner, more readable output.
help.hint_1: Store the input in a variable first.

mp-3-clean-typed-name.starterCode: # Read a raw name, clean it, and print it.

mp-4-build-full-profile-card.title: Build the full profile card
mp-4-build-full-profile-card.prompt:
Build the finished profile card.

Read three inputs in this order:
1. name
2. age
3. city

Then print exactly:
Profile for <clean name>
Age next year: <age + 1>
City: <city>

Clean the name with `.strip()` and `.title()`, convert the age to an integer, and use an f-string for the first line.
mp-4-build-full-profile-card.hint: Read the three inputs, clean the name, convert the age, then print the three report lines in order.

help.concept: The final profile card combines the main module skills: variables, input, type conversion, string methods, and readable formatting.
help.hint_1: Use `int(input())` for the age so you can add 1.
help.hint_2: The first line should use the cleaned name, not the raw input.

mp-4-build-full-profile-card.starterCode:
# Read the name, age, and city.
# Clean the name, update the age for next year, and print the profile card.

try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.title: Preview the profile card
try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.prompt: Store `"Ava"` in a variable named `name` and print it.
try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.hint: Assign first, then print the variable.

help.concept: This quick preview gives the project one clean stored value before the full profile card grows around it.
help.hint_2: Print the variable, not the text directly.

try-module-1-profile-card-project-sketch.starterCode:
# Store Ava in name.
# Then print name.

try_module_1_profile_card_project_sketch.title: Try it yourself
try_module_1_profile_card_project_sketch.prompt: Store `"Ava"` in a variable named `name` and print it.

sketch-1.title: Your first profile-card client
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A tutor is meeting several new students and wants a tiny profile card generator instead of rewriting the same information by hand every time.

This module project turns variables, input, and strings into one small interactive tool. Each step starts from a working version and adds a new improvement, so the final result feels like one complete beginner program.
